long_stay: Language courses (if over 90 days and at least 15-20 hours/week)
short_stay: Courses or work experience lasting less than 3 months
qualifying_courses.notes: Doctoral students may apply for a Talent Passport visa 'chercheur' instead of a standard student visa.

types.name: Long-stay Student Visa (VLS-TS)
types.notes: Valid for 4 months to 1 year, doubles as a residence permit upon validation.

types.name: Temporary Long-stay Visa (VLS-T)
types.notes: For stays of 4 months to 1 year only; not renewable.

booking_methods.description: Mandatory for eligible countries (e.g., UK, US, Canada, India) for academic verification before visa application.
Official online application portal for all visa types. For countries not using EEF, applications start here.
Authorized outsourced visa application centers for in-person appointments, document submission, and biometrics.
Mandatory online appointment system for all visa applications.

steps.title: Get Accepted by a French Institution
steps.description: Obtain your official enrollment or pre-enrollment letter.

steps.title: Register on Etudes en France (Campus France)
steps.description: Complete the academic verification process if your country requires it.

steps.title: Complete Online Visa Application
steps.description: Fill out the application form on france-visas.gouv.fr.

steps.title: Book In-Person Appointment
steps.description: Schedule an appointment at a visa application center or consulate.

steps.title: Attend Appointment and Submit Documents
steps.description: Bring all required documents (originals and copies) and register your biometrics.

steps.title: Pay Visa Fee
steps.description: Pay the application fee at the visa center.

steps.title: Wait for Processing and Passport Return
steps.description: Monitor your application status and await the return of your passport with the visa.

required_documents.name: Valid Passport
required_documents.details: Valid for at least 3 months beyond intended stay, issued within last 10 years, with at least 2 blank pages.

required_documents.name: Passport-sized Photos
required_documents.details: Two recent photos (35x45mm, white background, taken within last 6 months) matching Schengen standards.

required_documents.name: France-Visas Application Form
required_documents.details: Printed form (Cerfa n°14571*05) with barcode and registration receipt, generated from the online portal.

required_documents.name: Official Acceptance/Enrollment Letter
required_documents.details: From a recognized French educational institution (attestation d'inscription or pre-enrollment certificate).

required_documents.name: Proof of Financial Means
required_documents.details: Demonstrating at least €615 per month for the duration of stay.

required_documents.name: Proof of Accommodation
required_documents.details: For at least the first three months of stay (e.g., rental agreement, student housing confirmation, attestation d’hébergement).

required_documents.name: Proof of Health Insurance
required_documents.details: Valid in France with minimum coverage of €30,000. Students can enroll in French social security upon arrival.

required_documents.name: Language Proficiency Certificates
required_documents.details: DELF/DALF for French-taught programs, IELTS/TOEFL for English-taught programs.

required_documents.name: Proof of Legal Residency
required_documents.details: If country of residency differs from citizenship.

required_documents.name: CVEC Fee Payment Certificate
required_documents.details: Proof of payment for the Student Campus Life Contribution (~€105), mandatory for enrollment.

required_documents.name: Translated Documents
required_documents.details: All documents in a foreign language must be translated into French by an official translator and sometimes authenticated (apostille/notarization).

financial_requirements.reference_basis: Official minimum requirement for student visa applicants.
acceptable_proof: Bank statements (showing regular, reliable balances over last 3-6 months)
Sponsorship letter/affidavit of support (with sponsor's bank statements and proof of income)
exemptions: Students who have passed the entrance exam to a higher education institution that has signed an agreement with the State

residence_permit.authority_full_name: Administration Numérique des Étrangers en France
residence_permit.notes: Upon arrival with a VLS-TS visa, students must validate it online via the ANEF portal within 3 months. This validation costs €150 (tax and stamp duty combined) and functions as the legal residence permit for the first year. A separate Carte de Séjour is required from the second academic year, applied for at the local Préfecture before the VLS-TS expires.

restrictions: Algerian nationals are limited to 50% of normal working hours (approx. 482 hours/year) under the Franco-Algerian agreement.
work_while_studying.notes: Part-time work is a secondary source of income. The 2026 French minimum wage (SMIC) is €12.02 gross/hour (approx. €9.52 net/hour). Registering for social security is free and mandatory for non-EU students under 28, reimbursing ~60% of medical costs.

required_documents: Proof of address (dated within last 3 months)
Academic transcripts, attendance records, exam results, or diplomas obtained (to demonstrate serious progress in studies)
permit_renewal.notes: Applications must be submitted online via the ANEF portal between 4 and 2 months before your current permit expires. Late submission incurs a €180 penalty in addition to the residence permit fee. The first multi-annual residence permit will have a validity corresponding to the remaining years of your study program.

post_study_work.legal_basis: RECE (Recherche d'Emploi ou Création d'Entreprise) residence permit
requirements: Hold a Master's-level degree or equivalent (e.g., Engineering, IEP, higher accounting/management, veterinary, Licence Professionnelle)
Apply before the student residence permit expires
For direct change of status: secure a job (CDI or CDD of at least 12 months) related to studies, paying at least 1.5 times the SMIC (approx. €2,702 gross/month or €38,474.90 per year).
post_study_work.notes: The RECE permit is valid for one year and is non-renewable. If employment is secured or a company established during this year, a residence card in a professional capacity can be applied for before the RECE permit expires.

tips.title: Apply Early
tips.body: Begin your visa application at least 4-5 months before your course start date, especially if the Etudes en France (EEF) procedure is required, to account for processing times and potential delays.

tips.title: Validate Your VLS-TS Visa
tips.body: Upon arrival in France, validate your Long-stay Student Visa (VLS-TS) online via the ANEF portal within three months of entry to legalize your stay.

tips.title: Adhere to Residence Permit Renewal Deadlines
tips.body: Apply for residence permit renewal online via the ANEF portal between 4 and 2 months before your current permit expires to avoid a €180 penalty.

tips.title: Ensure Sufficient Financial Proof
tips.body: Your bank statements must clearly demonstrate access to at least €615 per month for your entire stay. Vague or low balances are a common reason for visa refusal.

tips.title: Provide Clear Accommodation Proof
tips.body: Ensure your housing certificate or rental contract explicitly covers at least your initial 90 days in France.

tips.title: Translate All Foreign Documents
tips.body: All documents not in French must be translated by an official translator and, in some cases, authenticated (e.g., with an apostille or notarization).

tips.title: Complete Campus France Registration
tips.body: If your country requires it, ensure you complete the pre-consular interview via the Etudes en France platform, as skipping this step invalidates your visa application.

tips.title: Timely RECE Permit Application
tips.body: Apply for the post-study 'Recherche d'Emploi ou Création d'Entreprise' (RECE) permit while you still hold, or have very recently held, valid French student residence status to avoid refusal.

tips.title: Avoid Unexplained Large Bank Deposits
tips.body: Consulates look for stable, regular funds. Large, unexplained lump sums deposited just before applying can raise red flags and lead to refusal.

tips.title: Pay the CVEC Fee
tips.body: The Student Campus Life Contribution (CVEC, ~€105) is mandatory for enrollment in French higher education. Ensure you pay it and have proof.

common_mistakes.mistake: Applying too late for the visa.
common_mistakes.consequence: Missing orientation, delays due to busy seasonal processing times, or visa refusal.
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Start your application at least 4-5 months before your course start date, especially if the EEF procedure is required.

common_mistakes.mistake: Not validating the VLS-TS visa after arrival.
common_mistakes.consequence: Your stay in France becomes illegal.
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Validate your visa online via the ANEF portal within three months of entry.

common_mistakes.mistake: Missing the residence permit renewal deadline.
common_mistakes.consequence: Incurs a €180 penalty fee on top of standard fees.
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Apply online via the ANEF portal between 4 and 2 months before your current permit expires.

common_mistakes.mistake: Entering France on a tourist visa with the intention to study.
common_mistakes.consequence: Illegal stay, subject to deportation proceedings.
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Always apply for the appropriate long-stay student visa (VLS-TS) before traveling.

common_mistakes.mistake: Providing insufficient or vague proof of financial means.
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Ensure bank statements clearly show at least €615 per month available for your stay, with stable and regular funds.
All documents in a foreign language must be translated into French by an official translator and sometimes authenticated (e.g., with an apostille).
Complete the pre-consular interview via the Etudes en France platform if your country is part of the program.

common_mistakes.mistake: Waiting too long to apply for the post-study RECE permit.
common_mistakes.consequence: Refusal if applied after a period of overstay.
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Apply for the RECE permit while you still hold, or have very recently held, valid French student residence status.

common_mistakes.mistake: Unexplained large bank deposits in financial proof.
common_mistakes.consequence: Raises a red flag and often triggers a refusal.
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Consulates look for stable, regular funds over several months, not sudden lump sums.

common_mistakes.mistake: Providing vague or insufficient proof of accommodation.
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Ensure your housing certificate or rental contract clearly covers at least your initial 90 days in France.

common_mistakes.mistake: Not paying the CVEC (Student Campus Life Contribution) fee.
common_mistakes.consequence: Mandatory for enrollment in French higher education, leading to issues with university registration.
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Pay the CVEC fee (~€105) and obtain proof of payment before your university enrollment.
